item.title: Nature of magnetic exchange interactions in the kagome antiferromagnets FeGe and FeSn
item.description:
Author(s): Yitao Zheng, Yan Zhu, and Jun Hu

Magnetic exchange interactions (MEIs) in kagome magnets exhibit rich features due to the interplay of charge, spin, orbital and lattice degrees of freedom, giving rise to a variety of exotic quantum states. Through first-principles calculations, we systematically investigate the MEIs in kagome antif…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094445] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yitao Zheng, Yan Zhu, and Jun Hu

Magnetic exchange interactions (MEIs) in kagome magnets exhibit rich features due to the interplay of charge, spin, orbital and lattice degrees of freedom, giving rise to a variety of exotic quantum states. Through first-principles calculations, we systematically investigate the MEIs in kagome antif…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094445] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Nature of magnetic exchange interactions in the kagome antiferromagnets FeGe and FeSn
item.dc:creator: Yitao Zheng, Yan Zhu, and Jun Hu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Conventional superconductivity in single-crystalline BiPt
item.description:
Author(s): S. Sharma, M. Pula, Sajilesh K. P., J. Gautreau, B. S. Agboola, J. P. Clancy, J. E. Sonier, A. Ghara, S. R. Dunsiger, M. Greven, M. J. Lagos, A. Kanigel, and G. M. Luke

Binary Bi-Pd/Pt systems have attracted a lot of interest because of their topologically nontrivial nature along with superconductivity. We report the structural and superconducting properties of high-quality single-crystalline BiPt using a comprehensive range of experimental techniques, including X-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094522] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. Sharma, M. Pula, Sajilesh K. P., J. Gautreau, B. S. Agboola, J. P. Clancy, J. E. Sonier, A. Ghara, S. R. Dunsiger, M. Greven, M. J. Lagos, A. Kanigel, and G. M. Luke

Binary Bi-Pd/Pt systems have attracted a lot of interest because of their topologically nontrivial nature along with superconductivity. We report the structural and superconducting properties of high-quality single-crystalline BiPt using a comprehensive range of experimental techniques, including X-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094522] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Conventional superconductivity in single-crystalline BiPt
item.dc:creator: S. Sharma, M. Pula, Sajilesh K. P., J. Gautreau, B. S. Agboola, J. P. Clancy, J. E. Sonier, A. Ghara, S. R. Dunsiger, M. Greven, M. J. Lagos, A. Kanigel, and G. M. Luke

item.title: Anisotropic fully gapped superconductivity in quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{Li}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{6}{\mathrm{O}}_{17}$
item.description:
Author(s): M. J. Grant, T. M. Huijbregts, R. Nicholls, M. Greenblatt, P. Chudzinski, A. Carrington, and N. E. Hussey

Superconductivity in quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{Li}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{6}{\mathrm{O}}_{17}$ emerges from an exotic, nonmetallic normal state that exhibits signatures of Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid behavior, emergent symmetry, and excitonic order. The high upper critical field, ${H}_{c2}$, in ${…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094524] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. J. Grant, T. M. Huijbregts, R. Nicholls, M. Greenblatt, P. Chudzinski, A. Carrington, and N. E. Hussey

Superconductivity in quasi-one-dimensional Li0.9Mo6O17 emerges from an exotic, nonmetallic normal state that exhibits signatures of Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid behavior, emergent symmetry, and excitonic order. The high upper critical field, Hc2, in Li0.9Mo6O17 suggests that that the favored pairing st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094524] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Anisotropic fully gapped superconductivity in quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{Li}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{6}{\mathrm{O}}_{17}$
item.dc:creator: M. J. Grant, T. M. Huijbregts, R. Nicholls, M. Greenblatt, P. Chudzinski, A. Carrington, and N. E. Hussey

item.title: Real-space atomic dynamics in liquid gallium studied by inelastic neutron scattering
item.description:
Author(s): Chengyun Hua, Yadu K. Sarathchandran, Eva Zarkadoula, Wojciech Dmowski, Douglas L. Abernathy, Takeshi Egami, and Yuya Shinohara

Gallium is a prototypical liquid metal and has gained renewed attention because of its unique properties. Characterizing and elucidating its atomic dynamics remains elusive despite numerous studies, primarily because of the challenges in quantifying atomic-scale dynamics in liquids. Recent developme…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104314] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chengyun Hua, Yadu K. Sarathchandran, Eva Zarkadoula, Wojciech Dmowski, Douglas L. Abernathy, Takeshi Egami, and Yuya Shinohara

Gallium is a prototypical liquid metal and has gained renewed attention because of its unique properties. Characterizing and elucidating its atomic dynamics remains elusive despite numerous studies, primarily because of the challenges in quantifying atomic-scale dynamics in liquids. Recent developme…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104314] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Real-space atomic dynamics in liquid gallium studied by inelastic neutron scattering
item.dc:creator: Chengyun Hua, Yadu K. Sarathchandran, Eva Zarkadoula, Wojciech Dmowski, Douglas L. Abernathy, Takeshi Egami, and Yuya Shinohara

item.title: Quantum phases in a two-dimensional generalized interacting Su-Schrieffer-Heeger model
item.description:
Author(s): Rahul Ghosh

We study interaction-driven quantum phases in a two-dimensional generalized Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) model defined on a square lattice with inequivalent nearest-neighbor hopping, next-nearest-neighbor hopping, and a staggered on-site potential. In the noninteracting limit, the model hosts either q…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115149] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rahul Ghosh

We study interaction-driven quantum phases in a two-dimensional generalized Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) model defined on a square lattice with inequivalent nearest-neighbor hopping, next-nearest-neighbor hopping, and a staggered on-site potential. In the noninteracting limit, the model hosts either q…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115149] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum phases in a two-dimensional generalized interacting Su-Schrieffer-Heeger model
item.dc:creator: Rahul Ghosh

item.title: Soft symmetries of topological orders
item.description:
Author(s): Ryohei Kobayashi and Maissam Barkeshli

(2+1)-dimensional [(2+1)D] topological orders possess emergent ordinary symmetries given by a group ${\text{Aut}}_{br}(\mathcal{C})$, which consists of the braided tensor autoequivalences of the modular tensor category $\mathcal{C}$ that describes the anyons. In this paper we discuss cases where ${\…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115150] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ryohei Kobayashi and Maissam Barkeshli

(2+1)-dimensional [(2+1)D] topological orders possess emergent ordinary symmetries given by a group Autbr(C), which consists of the braided tensor autoequivalences of the modular tensor category C that describes the anyons. In this paper we discuss cases where Autbr(C) has elements that neither perm…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115150] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Soft symmetries of topological orders
item.dc:creator: Ryohei Kobayashi and Maissam Barkeshli

item.title: Topological charge and bulk-surface correspondence for quad-helicoid surface states in topological semimetals with two glide-time-reversal symmetries
item.description:
Author(s): Taiki Yukitake and Shuichi Murakami

Quad-helicoid surface states (QHSSs) are unique surface states with two pairs of helicoid surface states in topological semimetals such as Dirac semimetals. So far, topologically protected QHSSs are shown to appear in spinless systems with two $\mathcal{GT}$ symmetries and $\mathcal{T}$ symmetry ($\…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115151] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Taiki Yukitake and Shuichi Murakami

Quad-helicoid surface states (QHSSs) are unique surface states with two pairs of helicoid surface states in topological semimetals such as Dirac semimetals. So far, topologically protected QHSSs are shown to appear in spinless systems with two GT symmetries and T symmetry (G: glide, T: time-reversal…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115151] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological charge and bulk-surface correspondence for quad-helicoid surface states in topological semimetals with two glide-time-reversal symmetries
item.dc:creator: Taiki Yukitake and Shuichi Murakami

item.title: Magnetocrystalline anisotropy and three-dimensional hopping conduction at the surface of ${\mathrm{FeSb}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Jarryd A. Horn, Yun Suk Eo, Keenan Avers, Hyeok Yoon, Ryan G. Dorman, Shanta R. Saha, and Johnpierre Paglione

Motivated by the recent discovery of metallic surface states in the $d$-electron Kondo insulator candidates FeSi and ${\mathrm{FeSb}}_{2}$, along with some recent reports of magnetic correlations in the surface transport properties of FeSi, we have investigated the low-temperature surface magnetotra…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125142] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jarryd A. Horn, Yun Suk Eo, Keenan Avers, Hyeok Yoon, Ryan G. Dorman, Shanta R. Saha, and Johnpierre Paglione

Motivated by the recent discovery of metallic surface states in the d-electron Kondo insulator candidates FeSi and FeSb2, along with some recent reports of magnetic correlations in the surface transport properties of FeSi, we have investigated the low-temperature surface magnetotransport properties …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125142] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetocrystalline anisotropy and three-dimensional hopping conduction at the surface of ${\mathrm{FeSb}}_{2}$

item.title: Bandwidth-enhanced moiré superchiral optical field in a nodal ring photonic crystal
item.description:
Author(s): Qiang Zhang, Ruiwen Chen, and Xiaocong Yuan

On-chip optical superchiral fields are usually generated using nanostructures. Here we demonstrate superchiral field generation using surface waves in a one-dimensional photonic crystal (PhC). The PhC supports nodal rings featuring a bandwidth-enhanced, all-angle degenerate dispersion for transverse…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125429] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qiang Zhang, Ruiwen Chen, and Xiaocong Yuan

On-chip optical superchiral fields are usually generated using nanostructures. Here we demonstrate superchiral field generation using surface waves in a one-dimensional photonic crystal (PhC). The PhC supports nodal rings featuring a bandwidth-enhanced, all-angle degenerate dispersion for transverse…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125429] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Bandwidth-enhanced moiré superchiral optical field in a nodal ring photonic crystal

item.title: Topological surface magnon-polariton in an insulating canted antiferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Weixin Li, Rundong Yuan, Fenglin Zhong, Bo Peng, Jean-Philippe Ansermet, and Haiming Yu

Excitation and control of antiferromagnetic magnon modes lie at the heart of coherent antiferromagnetic spintronics. Here, we propose a topological surface magnon-polariton as a new approach in the prototypical magnonic material hematite. We show that in an insulating canted antiferromagnet, where s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104439] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Weixin Li, Rundong Yuan, Fenglin Zhong, Bo Peng, Jean-Philippe Ansermet, and Haiming Yu

Excitation and control of antiferromagnetic magnon modes lie at the heart of coherent antiferromagnetic spintronics. Here, we propose a topological surface magnon-polariton as a new approach in the prototypical magnonic material hematite. We show that in an insulating canted antiferromagnet, where s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104439] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological surface magnon-polariton in an insulating canted antiferromagnet
item.dc:creator: Weixin Li, Rundong Yuan, Fenglin Zhong, Bo Peng, Jean-Philippe Ansermet, and Haiming Yu

item.title: Effects of next-nearest neighbor hopping on the pairing and critical temperatures of the attractive Hubbard model on a square lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Rodrigo A. Fontenele, Natanael C. Costa, Thereza Paiva, and Raimundo R. dos Santos

The attractive Hubbard model plays a paradigmatic role in the study of superconductivity (superfluidity) and has become directly realizable in ultracold atom experiments on optical lattices. However, the critical temperatures, ${T}_{c}$'s, remain lower than the lowest temperatures currently achievab…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104519] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rodrigo A. Fontenele, Natanael C. Costa, Thereza Paiva, and Raimundo R. dos Santos

The attractive Hubbard model plays a paradigmatic role in the study of superconductivity (superfluidity) and has become directly realizable in ultracold atom experiments on optical lattices. However, the critical temperatures, Tc's, remain lower than the lowest temperatures currently achievable in e…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104519] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Effects of next-nearest neighbor hopping on the pairing and critical temperatures of the attractive Hubbard model on a square lattice

item.title: Higher-dimensional overscreened fixed points and their deformations
item.description:
Author(s): Aleksandar Ljepoja, L. C. R. Wijewardhana, and Yashar Komijani

Non-Fermi liquids in dimensions $d>2$ remain poorly understood, particularly when relevant perturbations destabilize them. A prominent example is the overscreened Kondo fixed points in impurity models, and their one-dimensional analogs known as chirally stabilized fixed points. While such models …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115146] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026

Author(s): Aleksandar Ljepoja, L. C. R. Wijewardhana, and Yashar Komijani

Non-Fermi liquids in dimensions d>2 remain poorly understood, particularly when relevant perturbations destabilize them. A prominent example is the overscreened Kondo fixed points in impurity models, and their one-dimensional analogs known as chirally stabilized fixed points. While such models ca…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115146] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026

item.title: Charge carrier relaxation dynamics in the one-dimensional Kondo lattice model
item.description:
Author(s): Arturo Perez-Romero, Mica Schwarm, and Fabian Heidrich-Meisner

A generic question in the field of ultrafast dynamics is concerned with the relaxation dynamics and the subsequent thermalization of optically excited charge carriers. Among several possible relaxation channels available in a solid-state system, we focus on the coupling to magnetic excitations. In t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115147] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arturo Perez-Romero, Mica Schwarm, and Fabian Heidrich-Meisner

A generic question in the field of ultrafast dynamics is concerned with the relaxation dynamics and the subsequent thermalization of optically excited charge carriers. Among several possible relaxation channels available in a solid-state system, we focus on the coupling to magnetic excitations. In t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115147] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Charge carrier relaxation dynamics in the one-dimensional Kondo lattice model
item.dc:creator: Arturo Perez-Romero, Mica Schwarm, and Fabian Heidrich-Meisner
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Spin-dependent quasiparticle lifetimes in altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Kristoffer Leraand, Kristian Mæland, and Asle Sudbø

Altermagnets host spin-split electronic bands with zero magnetization. An important question is how robust this spin splitting is in the presence of many-body interactions. The authors show here how self-energy effects arising from interactions between electrons and three bosonic modes, namely phonons, magnons, and phonon-magnon hybridized modes, renormalize the electronic band structure in a minimal altermagnetic model. They find that the altermagnetic spin splitting remains spectroscopically resolvable despite the interaction-induced renormalization and broadening. Moreover, they uncover a distinct asymmetry in the magnon-induced quasiparticle broadening between the two electron spin species.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115148] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kristoffer Leraand, Kristian Mæland, and Asle Sudbø

Altermagnets host spin-split electronic bands with zero magnetization. An important question is how robust this spin splitting is in the presence of many-body interactions. The authors show here how self-energy effects arising from interactions between electrons and three bosonic modes, namely phonons, magnons, and phonon-magnon hybridized modes, renormalize the electronic band structure in a minimal altermagnetic model. They find that the altermagnetic spin splitting remains spectroscopically resolvable despite the interaction-induced renormalization and broadening. Moreover, they uncover a distinct asymmetry in the magnon-induced quasiparticle broadening between the two electron spin species.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115148] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-dependent quasiparticle lifetimes in altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Kristoffer Leraand, Kristian Mæland, and Asle Sudbø
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Dispersion of collective modes in spinful fractional quantum Hall states on the sphere
item.description:
Author(s): Rakesh K. Dora and Ajit C. Balram

Fractional quantum Hall (FQH) fluids are prototypical examples of topologically ordered systems. The addition of a flavor degree of freedom, such as spin, adds to the richness of the FQH phenomenology. Here, the authors compute the dispersion of various collective excitations in the most prominent spinless and spinful FQH states. Intriguingly, they find that certain spinful FQH states host a novel collective mode, which they explain via a parton construction. The predicted parton mode can be observed in light scattering experiments.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115420] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rakesh K. Dora and Ajit C. Balram

Fractional quantum Hall (FQH) fluids are prototypical examples of topologically ordered systems. The addition of a flavor degree of freedom, such as spin, adds to the richness of the FQH phenomenology. Here, the authors compute the dispersion of various collective excitations in the most prominent spinless and spinful FQH states. Intriguingly, they find that certain spinful FQH states host a novel collective mode, which they explain via a parton construction. The predicted parton mode can be observed in light scattering experiments.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115420] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Dispersion of collective modes in spinful fractional quantum Hall states on the sphere
item.dc:creator: Rakesh K. Dora and Ajit C. Balram
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Electric spin and valley tunneling Hall effects
item.description:
Author(s): W. Zeng

The electric Hall effect (EHE) is a newly identified Hall effect characterized by a perpendicular electric field inducing a transverse charge current in two-dimensional (2D) systems. Here, we propose a spin and valley version of EHE in ballistic tunnel junctions. We demonstrate that the transverse s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115421] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): W. Zeng

The electric Hall effect (EHE) is a newly identified Hall effect characterized by a perpendicular electric field inducing a transverse charge current in two-dimensional (2D) systems. Here, we propose a spin and valley version of EHE in ballistic tunnel junctions. We demonstrate that the transverse s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115421] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Electric spin and valley tunneling Hall effects
item.dc:creator: W. Zeng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Skin effect protected by custodial chiral symmetry
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoran Jing, Tian Chen, and Xiangdong Zhang

The non-Hermitian skin effect is a unique feature of non-Hermitian systems, in which an extensive number of boundary modes appear under open boundary conditions. It has been determined that this effect originates from intrinsic non-Hermitian topology protected by spatial symmetry or particle-hole sy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115423] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoran Jing, Tian Chen, and Xiangdong Zhang

The non-Hermitian skin effect is a unique feature of non-Hermitian systems, in which an extensive number of boundary modes appear under open boundary conditions. It has been determined that this effect originates from intrinsic non-Hermitian topology protected by spatial symmetry or particle-hole sy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115423] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026

item.title: Giant hysteretic magnetoresistance accompanying the Mott transition and spin-glass state in the organic metal $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)${}_{2}{\mathrm{Hg}(\mathrm{SCN})}_{2}\mathrm{Br}$
item.description:
Author(s): P. D. Grigoriev, S. I. Pesotskii, R. B. Lyubovskii, S. A. Torunova, D. S. Lyubshin, and V. N. Zverev

The giant magnetoresistance with a huge hysteresis is observed in the organic metal $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)${}_{2}{\mathrm{Hg}(\mathrm{SCN})}_{2}\mathrm{Br}$ at low temperature in a pressure interval around $3\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}\mathrm{kbar}$ of a width $∼1\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{kbar}$.…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125139] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): P. D. Grigoriev, S. I. Pesotskii, R. B. Lyubovskii, S. A. Torunova, D. S. Lyubshin, and V. N. Zverev

The giant magnetoresistance with a huge hysteresis is observed in the organic metal κ-(BEDT-TTF)2Hg(SCN)2Br at low temperature in a pressure interval around 3kbar of a width ∼1kbar. The hysteretic magnetoresistance is isotropic with respect to the direction of magnetic field, which excludes the orbi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125139] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Giant hysteretic magnetoresistance accompanying the Mott transition and spin-glass state in the organic metal $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)${}_{2}{\mathrm{Hg}(\mathrm{SCN})}_{2}\mathrm{Br}$
item.dc:creator: P. D. Grigoriev, S. I. Pesotskii, R. B. Lyubovskii, S. A. Torunova, D. S. Lyubshin, and V. N. Zverev

item.title: Su-Schrieffer-Heeger bipolaron in one dimension: Spectrum and dynamical response
item.description:
Author(s): Filip Marijanović, Yi-Fan Qu, and Eugene Demler

Characterizing bipolaron binding, and understanding how it depends on electron-phonon interaction, is crucial to unraveling the nature of emergent many-body states in strongly interacting electron-phonon systems. So far, most studies of bipolarons have been limited to the Holstein model, in which th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125140] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Filip Marijanović, Yi-Fan Qu, and Eugene Demler

Characterizing bipolaron binding, and understanding how it depends on electron-phonon interaction, is crucial to unraveling the nature of emergent many-body states in strongly interacting electron-phonon systems. So far, most studies of bipolarons have been limited to the Holstein model, in which th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125140] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Su-Schrieffer-Heeger bipolaron in one dimension: Spectrum and dynamical response
item.dc:creator: Filip Marijanović, Yi-Fan Qu, and Eugene Demler

item.title: Infinite-component $BF$ topological field theory: Connection of fracton order, Toeplitz braiding, and non-Hermitian amplification
item.description:
Author(s): Bo-Xi Li and Peng Ye

Building on the infinite-component Chern–Simons theory of three-dimensional fracton phases by Ma et al. [Phys. Rev. B 105, 195124 (2022)] and the Toeplitz braiding of anyons by Li et al. [Phys. Rev. B 110, 205108 (2024)], we show that stacking $(3+1)\mathrm{D} BF$ topological field theories, which s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125141] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bo-Xi Li and Peng Ye

Building on the infinite-component Chern–Simons theory of three-dimensional fracton phases by Ma et al. [Phys. Rev. B 105, 195124 (2022)] and the Toeplitz braiding of anyons by Li et al. [Phys. Rev. B 110, 205108 (2024)], we show that stacking (3+1)D BF topological field theories, which serve as low…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125141] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Infinite-component $BF$ topological field theory: Connection of fracton order, Toeplitz braiding, and non-Hermitian amplification
item.dc:creator: Bo-Xi Li and Peng Ye

item.title: Spin-selective higher-order topological corner states in a bilayer Kane-Mele-Haldane model
item.description:
Author(s): Yu-Long Zhang, Lizhou Liu, Qing-Feng Sun, Jian-Jun Liu, and Ying-Tao Zhang

We propose a bilayer Kane-Mele-Haldane model that realizes spin-selective higher-order topology and generates spin-polarized corner states without the need for external magnetic fields. A single layer combining intrinsic spin-orbit coupling, a Haldane mass, and a staggered sublattice potential suppo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125427] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yu-Long Zhang, Lizhou Liu, Qing-Feng Sun, Jian-Jun Liu, and Ying-Tao Zhang

We propose a bilayer Kane-Mele-Haldane model that realizes spin-selective higher-order topology and generates spin-polarized corner states without the need for external magnetic fields. A single layer combining intrinsic spin-orbit coupling, a Haldane mass, and a staggered sublattice potential suppo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125427] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-selective higher-order topological corner states in a bilayer Kane-Mele-Haldane model

item.title: Boundary criticality in two-dimensional interacting topological insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Yang Ge, Hong Yao, and Shao-Kai Jian

We study the boundary criticality in 2D interacting topological insulators. Using the determinant quantum Monte Carlo method, we present a nonperturbative study of the boundary quantum phase diagram in the Kane-Mele-Hubbard-Rashba model. Our results reveal rich boundary critical phenomena at the qua…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121108] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yang Ge, Hong Yao, and Shao-Kai Jian

We study the boundary criticality in 2D interacting topological insulators. Using the determinant quantum Monte Carlo method, we present a nonperturbative study of the boundary quantum phase diagram in the Kane-Mele-Hubbard-Rashba model. Our results reveal rich boundary critical phenomena at the qua…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121108] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Boundary criticality in two-dimensional interacting topological insulators
item.dc:creator: Yang Ge, Hong Yao, and Shao-Kai Jian

item.title: Sliding-tuned quantum geometry in moiré systems: Nonlinear Hall effect and quantum metric control
item.description:
Author(s): Shi-Ping Ding, Miao Liang, Tian-Le Wu, Meng-Hao Wu, Jing-Tao Lü, Jin-Hua Gao, and X. C. Xie

Sliding is a ubiquitous phenomenon in moiré systems, but its direct influence on moiré bands, especially in multitwist moiré systems, has been largely overlooked to date. Here, we theoretically show that sliding provides a unique pathway to engineer the quantum geometry (Berry curvature and quantum …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121411] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shi-Ping Ding, Miao Liang, Tian-Le Wu, Meng-Hao Wu, Jing-Tao Lü, Jin-Hua Gao, and X. C. Xie

Sliding is a ubiquitous phenomenon in moiré systems, but its direct influence on moiré bands, especially in multitwist moiré systems, has been largely overlooked to date. Here, we theoretically show that sliding provides a unique pathway to engineer the quantum geometry (Berry curvature and quantum …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121411] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026

item.title: Two-stage ferroelastic transition of stishovite due to one-dimensional hydrogen defects
item.description:
Author(s): Junwei Li, Sensen Wu, Haiyang Xian, Catalin Popescu, Davide Comboni, Michael I. Hanfland, Hongliang Dong, Binbin Yue, Yanhao Lin, and Qingyang Hu

The effects of one-dimensional hydrogen on the ferroelastic transition and elasticity of stishovite are determined up to 52.5 GPa using x-ray diffraction, Raman spectroscopy, and first-principles calculations. In contrast to the well-studied hydrogen-free stishovite or that with hydrogarnetlike poin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094111] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junwei Li, Sensen Wu, Haiyang Xian, Catalin Popescu, Davide Comboni, Michael I. Hanfland, Hongliang Dong, Binbin Yue, Yanhao Lin, and Qingyang Hu

The effects of one-dimensional hydrogen on the ferroelastic transition and elasticity of stishovite are determined up to 52.5 GPa using x-ray diffraction, Raman spectroscopy, and first-principles calculations. In contrast to the well-studied hydrogen-free stishovite or that with hydrogarnetlike poin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094111] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Two-stage ferroelastic transition of stishovite due to one-dimensional hydrogen defects

item.title: Compact localized fermions and Ising anyons in a chiral spin liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Tim Bauer and Johannes Reuther

Quasiparticle hybridization remains a major challenge in realizing and controlling exotic states of matter in existing quantum simulation platforms. We report the absence of hybridization for compact localized states (CLSs) emerging in the chiral spin liquid described by the Yao-Kivelson model. The …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094442] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tim Bauer and Johannes Reuther

Quasiparticle hybridization remains a major challenge in realizing and controlling exotic states of matter in existing quantum simulation platforms. We report the absence of hybridization for compact localized states (CLSs) emerging in the chiral spin liquid described by the Yao-Kivelson model. The …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094442] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: From quantum geometry to nonlinear optics and gerbes: Recent advances in topological band theory
item.description:
Author(s): Tomáš Bzdušek

This Perspective explores recent advances in topological band theory, focusing on the quantum geometric tensor, delicate and multigap topology, and higher-form structures like bundle gerbes. It highlights how these geometric features relate to first- and higher-order optical responses, including the quantization of shift currents. By moving beyond conventional Berryology, these threads offer new insights into discovery and materials design for electronic and synthetic condensed matter systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 099601] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tomáš Bzdušek

This Perspective explores recent advances in topological band theory, focusing on the quantum geometric tensor, delicate and multigap topology, and higher-form structures like bundle gerbes. It highlights how these geometric features relate to first- and higher-order optical responses, including the quantization of shift currents. By moving beyond conventional Berryology, these threads offer new insights into discovery and materials design for electronic and synthetic condensed matter systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 099601] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Unlocking doping effects on altermagnetism in MnTe: Emergence of quasi-altermagnetism
item.description:
Author(s): Nayana Devaraj, Anumita Bose, Arindom Das, Md Afsar Reja, Arijit Mandal, Awadhesh Narayan, and B. R. K. Nanda

Governed by specific symmetries, altermagnetism is an emerging field in condensed matter physics, characterized by unique spin-splitting of the bands in the momentum space coexisting with the compensated magnetization as in antiferromagnets. As crystals can have tailored and unintended defects, it i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104438] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

Governed by specific symmetries, altermagnetism is an emerging field in condensed matter physics, characterized by unique spin-splitting of the bands in the momentum space coexisting with the compensated magnetization as in antiferromagnets. As crystals can have tailored and unintended defects, it i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104438] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Ideal nodal-sphere semimetal in the three-dimensional boron allotrope ${\text{CT-B}}_{24}$
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao-Jing Gao, Yanfeng Ge, and Yan Gao

Nodal-sphere semimetals (NSSMs), featuring spherical band degeneracies in momentum space, constitute a fascinating class of topological materials. However, their realization in real materials is severely hampered by discrete crystallographic symmetry constraints, often resulting in gapped “pseudo” n…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115145] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

However, their realization in real materials is severely hampered by discrete crystallographic symmetry constraints, often resulting in gapped “pseudo” n…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115145] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Study of exciton-photon coupling in CuCl semiconductor microcavities with large decay of cavity photons
item.description:
Author(s): Rintaro Miyanaga, Goro Oohata, Satoshi Tanaka, and Kohji Mizoguchi

In CuCl semiconductor microcavities with large decay of the cavity photon (CP), we have studied the dependence of the coupling energy between excitons and CPs on the position of the active layer in the cavity layer, using angle-resolved transmission (ART) spectroscopy. The ART spectra show that the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115309] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Magnetotransport properties of an unconventional Rashba spin-orbit coupled two-dimensional electronic system
item.description:
Author(s): Aryan Pandita and SK Firoz Islam

We investigate the magnetotransport properties of a two-dimensional electronic system with unconventional Rashba spin-orbit coupling. We obtain the Landau levels analytically and find that intra-spin and/or inter-spin Landau-level crossing occurs. We compute the longitudinal conductivity and quantum…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115419] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

Author(s): Aryan Pandita and SK Firoz Islam

We investigate the magnetotransport properties of a two-dimensional electronic system with unconventional Rashba spin-orbit coupling. We obtain the Landau levels analytically and find that intra-spin and/or inter-spin Landau-level crossing occurs. We compute the longitudinal conductivity and quantum…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115419] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Interplay of Rashba and valley Zeeman splittings in weak localization of spin-orbit coupled graphene
item.description:
Author(s): L. E. Golub

Weak localization theory is developed for graphene heterostructures with transition metal dichalcogenides and topological insulators where the Rashba and valley Zeeman spin splittings of the energy spectrum are large enough. The anomalous magnetoresistance in low fields caused by weak localization i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125306] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

Weak localization theory is developed for graphene heterostructures with transition metal dichalcogenides and topological insulators where the Rashba and valley Zeeman spin splittings of the energy spectrum are large enough. The anomalous magnetoresistance in low fields caused by weak localization i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125306] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Universal classes of disorder scattering for the in-plane anomalous Hall effect
item.description:
Author(s): Guoao Yang, Tao Qin, and Jianhui Zhou

The in-plane anomalous Hall effect (IPAHE) with planar Hall current and magnetization/magnetic fields in various quantum materials has received increasing attention. Most of the current efforts are devoted to the intrinsic part due to the Berry curvature of electronic bands, however, how disorder sc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125426] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

Most of the current efforts are devoted to the intrinsic part due to the Berry curvature of electronic bands, however, how disorder sc…

item.title: Chiral spin liquid and quantum phase transition in the triangular-lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Stefan Divic, Tomohiro Soejima (副島智大), Valentin Crépel, Michael P. Zaletel, and Andrew Millis

Recent advances in moiré engineering motivate the study of lattice models of strongly correlated electrons subjected to substantial orbital magnetic flux. We analyze the triangular-lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model at one-quarter flux quantum per plaquette and a density of one electron per site, wher…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121107] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

We analyze the triangular-lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model at one-quarter flux quantum per plaquette and a density of one electron per site, wher…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121107] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Projectification of point group symmetries with a background flux and the Lieb-Schultz-Mattis theorem
item.description:
Author(s): Yasuhiro Tada and Masaki Oshikawa

We discuss the Lieb-Schultz-Mattis (LSM) theorem in two-dimensional spin systems with onsite $\mathrm{U}(1)⋊{\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ spin-rotation symmetry and point group ${C}_{2v}$ symmetry about a site. We “twist” the point group symmetry by introducing a small uniform U(1) flux to obtain a projective s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094439] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yasuhiro Tada and Masaki Oshikawa

We discuss the Lieb-Schultz-Mattis (LSM) theorem in two-dimensional spin systems with onsite U(1)⋊Z2 spin-rotation symmetry and point group C2v symmetry about a site. We “twist” the point group symmetry by introducing a small uniform U(1) flux to obtain a projective symmetry, similarly to the famili…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094439] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Projectification of point group symmetries with a background flux and the Lieb-Schultz-Mattis theorem

item.title: Singularities at load-induced moiré locking transitions in twist grain boundaries
item.description:
Author(s): Jin Wang and Erio Tosatti

Recent simulation work on the moiré formed by small angle crystalline twist grain boundaries (TGBs) revealed an unexpected load-induced first-order structural transition. Most likely universal, this unconventional transition drives a frictionally superlubric-to-locked transformation. It involves moi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104107] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Incommensurate twisted bilayer graphene: Emerging quasiperiodicity and stability
item.description:
Author(s): Ian Jauslin and Vieri Mastropietro

We consider a lattice model of twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) for incommensurate twist angles, focusing on the role of large-momentum-transfer umklapp terms. These terms, which nearly connect the Fermi points of different layers, are typically neglected in effective continuum descriptions but could,…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115142] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

These terms, which nearly connect the Fermi points of different layers, are typically neglected in effective continuum descriptions but could,…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115142] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Theoretical study of the origin of x-ray atomic background anomalies by a full-potential multiple scattering approach
item.description:
Author(s): Kazuki Yoshikawa, Fabio Iesari, Yoshiaki Tamura, Naoki Nakatani, Fukiko Ota, Andrea Di Cicco, and Keisuke Hatada

Previous extended x-ray absorption fine structure (EXAFS) studies have shown evidence of anomalous contributions in the atomic background function whose origin was debated in the literature. The present study presents advanced multiple scattering calculations aimed to put to a test the existence and…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115143] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

The present study presents advanced multiple scattering calculations aimed to put to a test the existence and…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115143] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Theoretical study of the origin of x-ray atomic background anomalies by a full-potential multiple scattering approach

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Intrinsic nonlinear Hall effect beyond Bloch geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Raffaele Resta

The intrinsic Hall effect, both linear and nonlinear, is a perspicuous manifestation of quantum geometry. In the current literature, the formulation is based on Bloch geometry, addressing crystalline systems in a mean-field framework. Nonetheless, the effect is a fundamental geometric response of a generic many-body ground state, not a band structure peculiarity. The author provides here an higher-level quantum-geometrical theory of the quadratic Hall effect, cast in very compact notations, and encompassing the known Bloch formulation as a special case.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125136] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Raffaele Resta

The intrinsic Hall effect, both linear and nonlinear, is a perspicuous manifestation of quantum geometry. In the current literature, the formulation is based on Bloch geometry, addressing crystalline systems in a mean-field framework. Nonetheless, the effect is a fundamental geometric response of a generic many-body ground state, not a band structure peculiarity. The author provides here an higher-level quantum-geometrical theory of the quadratic Hall effect, cast in very compact notations, and encompassing the known Bloch formulation as a special case.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125136] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Intrinsic nonlinear Hall effect beyond Bloch geometry
item.dc:creator: Raffaele Resta
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Robust semiclassical magnetization plateau in the kagome lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriel Capelo and Eric C. Andrade

Inspired by recent observations of the $1/3$ magnetization plateau in kagome-based magnets, we investigate the ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Heisenberg model on the kagome lattice under the influence of an external magnetic field. Although the classical ground state at zero field depends on the sign of $…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125137] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriel Capelo and Eric C. Andrade

Inspired by recent observations of the 1/3 magnetization plateau in kagome-based magnets, we investigate the J1−J2 Heisenberg model on the kagome lattice under the influence of an external magnetic field. Although the classical ground state at zero field depends on the sign of J2, we find a robust 1…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125137] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Tailoring corner states and exceptional points in altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao-Ming Zhao, Cui-Xian Guo, Xin-Ran Ma, Xiao-Ran Wang, and Su-Peng Kou

Altermagnets (AMs) exhibit vanishing net magnetization but strong momentum-dependent spin splitting enforced by crystal symmetry. Here, we explore the non-Hermitian (NH) effects in dissipative two-dimensional AMs. We show that symmetry-compliant dissipation naturally induces an imaginary staggered e…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100409] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao-Ming Zhao, Cui-Xian Guo, Xin-Ran Ma, Xiao-Ran Wang, and Su-Peng Kou

Altermagnets (AMs) exhibit vanishing net magnetization but strong momentum-dependent spin splitting enforced by crystal symmetry. Here, we explore the non-Hermitian (NH) effects in dissipative two-dimensional AMs. We show that symmetry-compliant dissipation naturally induces an imaginary staggered e…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100409] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Tailoring corner states and exceptional points in altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Xiao-Ming Zhao, Cui-Xian Guo, Xin-Ran Ma, Xiao-Ran Wang, and Su-Peng Kou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Interlayer sliding and two-stage superconductivity in pressurized $\mathrm{N}{\mathrm{b}}_{0.6}\mathrm{R}{\mathrm{e}}_{0.4}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Can Tian, Chenchen Liu, Wuhao Chen, Yuchen Zhang, Yuqiang Fang, and Xiaoli Huang

Here, the authors report a pressure-driven interlayer sliding in Nb0.6Re0.4Se2 that induces an unusual two-stage superconductivity. Synchrotron x-ray diffraction and Raman spectroscopy reveal an isosymmetric 2Hc-2Ha structural transition accompanied by phonon softening. Superconductivity emerges near the critical pressure Pc and develops a nonmonotonic phase diagram, characterized by a prominent superconducting dome followed by a slow reentrant enhancement at higher pressures. The findings highlight interlayer sliding as an effective route to engineer complex superconducting states in layered materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094516] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Can Tian, Chenchen Liu, Wuhao Chen, Yuchen Zhang, Yuqiang Fang, and Xiaoli Huang

Here, the authors report a pressure-driven interlayer sliding in Nb0.6Re0.4Se2 that induces an unusual two-stage superconductivity. Synchrotron x-ray diffraction and Raman spectroscopy reveal an isosymmetric 2Hc-2Ha structural transition accompanied by phonon softening. Superconductivity emerges near the critical pressure Pc and develops a nonmonotonic phase diagram, characterized by a prominent superconducting dome followed by a slow reentrant enhancement at higher pressures. The findings highlight interlayer sliding as an effective route to engineer complex superconducting states in layered materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094516] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Interlayer sliding and two-stage superconductivity in pressurized $\mathrm{N}{\mathrm{b}}_{0.6}\mathrm{R}{\mathrm{e}}_{0.4}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Can Tian, Chenchen Liu, Wuhao Chen, Yuchen Zhang, Yuqiang Fang, and Xiaoli Huang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Hybrid collective excitations in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): T. Karabassov, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, A. S. Vasenko, and A. A. Golubov

We develop a linear response theory for the dynamical proximity effect in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator (TS/FI) hybrids. Our approach integrates the nonequilibrium quasiclassical Keldysh-Usadel formalism for the TS with the Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert equation for the FI's magnetiza…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104514] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. Karabassov, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, A. S. Vasenko, and A. A. Golubov

We develop a linear response theory for the dynamical proximity effect in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator (TS/FI) hybrids. Our approach integrates the nonequilibrium quasiclassical Keldysh-Usadel formalism for the TS with the Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert equation for the FI's magnetiza…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104514] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Hybrid collective excitations in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator heterostructures
item.dc:creator: T. Karabassov, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, A. S. Vasenko, and A. A. Golubov

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity
